title: senecio bridgesii
intro: Senecio bridgesii, also known as Hooker's ragwort, is a flowering plant that belongs to the daisy family and is native to New Zealand. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. Senecio bridgesii is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and acting as an expectorant to help clear mucus from the lungs. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, but its use should be approached with caution due to potential toxicity and liver damage if ingested in large quantities.
